Electronic cigarettes, also known e-cigarettes, battery-operated devices that have cartridges with liquid chemicals them.
Heat from battery powered atomizer turns chemicals into vapour that inhaled user (called vaping).
E-cigarettes look like regular cigarettes.
There other electronic smoking products that look like cigars pipes.
Some e-cigarettes reusable.
cartridges reusable e-cigarettes replaced filled with liquid (sold separately).
also disposable e-cigarettes.
What e-cigarette liquid?
There standards labelling requirements e-cigarettes.
This makes hard know exactly what liquid.
Common contents include water propylene glycol, man-made product used antifreeze theatre fog.
Many e-cigarettes also have chemicals them that give vapour flavour.
Although e-cigarettes promoted being safe they haven't been tested safety.
There quality controls making e-cigarettes.
Because chemicals cartridges vary, it's hard know what e-cigarette users people nearby breathing
Promotional materials e-cigarettes describe vapor water vapor.
However, U.S. Food Drug Administration found cancer-causing chemicals some cartridges.
It's known vaporized propylene glycol other chemicals cartridges safe inhale.
Given that e-cigarettes don't seem create 7,000 chemical which known cause cancer) found cigarette smoke they likely safer than smoking regular cigarette; however, that doesn't mean they safe use.
studies done e-cigarettes researchers found signs airways becoming narrow inflammation after using e-cigarette only minutes.
Health Canada advises e-cigarettes because they haven't been tested harmful.
Health Canada also warns that e-cigarettes cause nicotine poisoning addiction.
Health Canada reminds people keep products away from children prevent nicotine poisoning choking.
E-cigarettes e-liquids that contain nicotine that promoted with therapeutic health claim (for example, that they help people quit smoking) regulated health products that have authorized Health Canada before they legally sold Canada.
date, products have been authorized.
Please call Health Canada toll-free 1-800-267-9675 would like more information about legality e-cigarettes.
they smoked anywhere, anytime?
There growing trend smoking e-cigarettes indoors.
Many health organizations support sale e-cigarettes until more research health effects done.
E-cigarettes used Alberta Health Services (AHS) property.
property means grounds, facilities, work vehicles personal vehicles AHS' grounds parking lots.
There evidence that using e-cigarettes helps people quit smoking.
Health Canada, U.S. Food Drug Administration, World Health organization don't support e-cigarettes stop-smoking products.
Health Canada approved stop-smoking medicine, including over-the-counter nicotine patches, gum, lozenges, inhaler.
Prescription medicine also available.
These products have been proven safe help tobacco users reduce quit.
Alberta Health Services gives support information people interested quitting smoking.

This course aims familiarize students with three forms nineteenth-century British writing.
first sub-category Victorian novel "industrial" "social problem" novel which appeared around mid-century response contemporary crises class-relations.
Industrial novelists from Dickens Disraeli used fiction both stage which dramatize plight working class, laboratory which develop some possible antidotes.
discussing industrial novel, will look closely middle-class upper-class writers represented working-class characters, their rhetorical handling class conflict, plots their novels imagine romantic, narrative, affective solutions widespread structural socio-economic problems.
will examine both power limitations aesthetic traditionally space consolation distraction rather than agitation rupture representing suffering compelling readers demand political economic change.
Having asked such questions working-class representation, will turn genres self-representation, particularly working-class diaries autobiographies.
Victorian working-class writers construct their subjectivities without, even against, strategies conventions middle-class fiction autobiography?
these autobiographies diaries construct writing self differently from their middle-class analogues?
such texts frustrate alter novel- memoir-reader's hunger closure, symmetry, suspense, diversion?
might such frustrations alterations have been, politically productive?
Finally, will look several nineteenth-century political social scientists (particularly Engels Mayhew) used emerging sociological political economic methods chronicle lives working poor.
Their texts will help central questions course: what relationship between manner matter, between taxonomy lived reality that between classification class?
This question will lead into terms "material," "materiality," "materialism" their many tangled senses physical (material versus spiritual), industrial (raw material, yards material), philosophical historiographic (materialism versus idealism), legal ("material testimony"), gendered such etymological conceptual cognates "maternal" "matrix").
Over course semester, will look different ways Victorian writers bodied forth their ideas about materiality print; materiality signifier; relations among things, objects, stuff, property; relations between "realism" "real."
will consider what material (again, senses) conditions govern acts reading, speaking, getting, giving, spending, working.
Primary readings from among: Dickens, Gaskell, Kingsley, Disraeli, George Eliot, George Moore; William Dodd, Hannah Cullwick, Emma Smith; Marx, Engels, Mayhew.

Mendip Hills most southerly Carboniferous Limestone upland Britain.
Carboniferous Limestones were laid down during early Carboniferous period, about 320-350 million years ago.
Subsequently, much northwestern Europe underwent continental collision throughout late Paleozoic era, culminating final phases Variscan orogeny near Carboniferous period, million years ago.
This tectonic activity produced complex suite mountain hill ranges across what southern Ireland, south-western England, Brittany, elsewhere western Europe.
result Variscan mountain-building, Mendip area comprises least four anticlinal fold structures, with east-west trend, each with core older Devonian sandstone Silurian volcanic rocks.
latter quarried road construction concrete aggregate.
Mendip considerably higher steeper million years ago, since when weathering resulted range surface features including gorges, valleys, screes swallets.
These complemented underground large number caves, including Wookey Hole, both beneath plateau base southern escarpment.
There also limestone pavements other karst features.
Dissolution limestone produced many gorges including, most famously, Cheddar Gorge Burrington Combe.
Springs, number which deposit tufa, particular feature eastern part hills.
